reviews.review_msg: Awful ! horrible! high school attitude. Things too much of her self.  Unfair and unethical. Never take  this course with her.

Even though the content is very straight forward, she tries to give a lot of good examples in class, which makes it less boring. She is really nice and always willing to help. The midterm and final are multiple choice and are easy.

She is so useless that I pass my course by looking at slides and spending 10 hrs max. You can imagine how non sense the course material was. She is all over the place with her high school attitude. I mean come on grow up you are a PHD student yourself and still act like a teenager. SFU is that desperate to let these people teach ?!!
Do the work and receive a good grade. All multiple choice exams. Karen is super helpful and just wants everyone to participate in class.

Karen is pretty knowledgeable in marketing. She does a good job explaining concepts and reinforces the learning through good examples. Her exams are only multiple-choice, so read the textbook and follow her lecture slides to do well. I personally enjoyed the personal branding pitch as I learned a lot from the industry professional I had.
boring lecture and difficult exams, do not take with her

lecture is boring but required if you want to pass this course, individual is irreverent to the content. Always reading from lecture, you need to read both the lecture notes and textbooks if you want to success in exam.

Lectures are boring, but essential if you want to do well. Assignments are only somewhat related to lecture content, and the criteria is extremely vague. Midterm and final are all MC, so you need to spend a lot of time memorizing terms. Also, a big portion of your grade is a group report and presentation.

Reads of her slides too often, thus creating lectures that are fairly dull. However you basically need to attend them since she asks questions on the MT about her lectures. MT was all MC and so is the final. Fairly easy but overall did not enjoy the course through her teaching. Assignments will differentiate your letter grade in the class.
